core_model.structure_summary: A first-order-in-time scalar evolution equation with a nonlinear (quadratic) flux and a dissipative fractional-order smoothing operator, admitting self-similar single-signed source-type solutions of the form u(x,t)=t^{-1/2}U(x/t^{1/2}).

variables.meaning: scalar field evolving in one spatial dimension
dissipation strength and fractional dissipation order (criticality)

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A scalar conservation law with quadratic nonlinear flux and a dissipative term, stripped of the self-similar/near-identity change of variables.

notes: Core is the (hypoviscous) Burgers equation at criticality; a nonlocal/fractional dissipation replaces the standard Laplacian, and the paper's later sections extend the same nonlinear-transport-plus-dissipation structure to a 2D active-scalar (SQG-type) equation with a singular integral (Hilbert/Riesz-transform) velocity, which shares the nonlinear-flux + dissipation skeleton. Extraction noise present in the display equations; identification rests mainly on title/abstract.
